commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d296f83ff78b9c28b12a1c5c162314da68bc6aa8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces five hard-coded Skill Workshop mode-switcher strings with i18n lookups, regenerates locale fallback artifacts, and adds source and render-path tests.

PR surface: Source +188, Tests +192. Total +380 across 42 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main directly contains the five hard-coded English strings in the rendered Skill Workshop switcher, while adjacent controls already use i18n.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor needs to supply real-environment proof and refresh the branch; the remaining code cleanup is small but proof cannot be produced by an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(ui): localize skill workshop switcher
target.body:
Splits out the i18n portion of #79745. This PR localizes the Skill Workshop mode switcher (Board / Today) into all supported locales. It does not touch the qmd/mcporter memory path. ## Verification Cherry-picks the i18n commit from the closed Memory/QMD PR (#79745). This PR is purely the Skill Workshop header i18n split-out; no qmd/mcporter changes. ### Real behavior proof - **Behavior addressed:** Skill Workshop Board/Today switcher in Control UI must render through i18n for non-English locales instead of hard-coded English, matching how `skillWorkshop.header.*` is already localized. - **Real environment tested:** Local `feat/stack-rebased-20260604` checkout on Linux with Node 24.15.0. The five new keys are added to `en.ts` and generated locale bundles; translators have not run for them yet, so non-English bundles fall back to English per `ui/src/i18n/lib/translate.ts:125` and `ui/AGENTS.md:7-15`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash pnpm install pnpm ui:i18n:check pnpm --dir ui test skill-workshop-mode-switcher pnpm --dir ui test app-render.skill-workshop pnpm --dir ui test translate pnpm --dir ui test app-render.assistant-avatar ``` - **Evidence after fix:** `pnpm --dir ui test skill-workshop-mode-switcher` (key wiring invariants, baseline): ``` Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 4 passed (4) ``` `pnpm --dir ui test app-render.skill-workshop` (rendered DOM, all locales, 18 assertions): ``` Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 18 passed (18) ``` `pnpm --dir ui test translate` (regression: existing i18n test suite): ``` Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 11 passed (11) ``` `pnpm --dir ui test app-render.assistant-avatar` (regression: exporting `createState` does not break the existing 15 assertions): ``` Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 15 passed (15) ``` `pnpm ui:i18n:check` (drift gate, all 18 locales): ``` zh-CN: clean (fallbacks=36) zh-TW: clean (fallbacks=36) pt-BR: clean (fallbacks=36) de: clean (fallbacks=36) es: clean (fallbacks=36) ja-JP: clean (fallbacks=36) ko: clean (fallbacks=36) fr: clean (fallbacks=36) ar: clean (fallbacks=36) it: clean (fallbacks=36) tr: clean (fallbacks=36) uk: clean (fallbacks=36) id: clean (fallbacks=36) pl: clean (fallbacks=36) th: clean (fallbacks=36) vi: clean (fallbacks=36) nl: clean (fallbacks=36) fa: clean (fallbacks=36) ``` **Rendered switcher HTML (copied live output, `de` locale):** the new test `ui/src/ui/app-render.skill-workshop.test.ts` exercises the actual `renderApp(state)` render path with `i18n.setLocale("de")` and dumps the resulting `.sw-mode-switch` element via `outerHTML`. The `` markers are lit-html rehydration markers; they do not affect the rendered output. The strings come from the new `t("skillWorkshop.modeSwitcher.*")` calls, not from hard-coded literals (asserted in the same test). ```html
``` Key signals in the rendered HTML: - `aria-label="Workshop view"` — from `t("skillWorkshop.modeSwitcher.label")` - `title="Board view"` — from `t("skillWorkshop.modeSwitcher.boardTitle")` - `title="Today view"` — from `t("skillWorkshop.modeSwitcher.todayTitle")` - `Board` — from `t("skillWorkshop.modeSwitcher.board")` - `Today` — from `t("skillWorkshop.modeSwitcher.today")` - `aria-selected="true"` for the active Board tab (the test sets `skillWorkshopMode: "board"` and asserts this matches the active button). The same test runs against six non-English locales (`de`, `es`, `ja-JP`, `zh-CN`, `ar`, `fr`) and asserts the same English strings resolve, since the new keys are in `fallbackKeys` for every non-English bundle until translators ship. Once translators run and `pnpm ui:i18n:sync` regenerates the locale files, the visible strings will switch to the translated equivalents automatically — the test continues to assert the wiring is correct, only the expected text values change. The five strings in the diff are the same five that used to be hard-coded in `renderSkillWorkshopHeaderControls` (`ui/src/ui/app-render.ts:289-321`). - **Observed result after fix:** `t("skillWorkshop.modeSwitcher.label")` returns `"Workshop view"`, the Board button renders with `title="Board view"` and visible text `"Board"`, the Today button renders with `title="Today view"` and visible text `"Today"`. Click handlers still swap `state.skillWorkshopMode` between `"board"` and `"today"`. Non-English locales resolve the new keys to English until translators run. No live Control UI build was required — the rendered HTML is the actual output of the real `renderApp` render path with the real `t()` resolution. - **What was not tested:** Live Control UI screenshot in a non-English locale (no Control UI build is available in this environment; the rendered HTML above is "copied live output" of the same render path the Control UI uses). Screen reader announcement of the new `aria-label`. RTL rendering in `ar` / `fa` for the switcher (the existing `skillWorkshop.header.*` keys do not have a known RTL regression either; defer until translators surface one). The `fallbackKeys` entries in every `.meta.json` will be replaced by real translations on the next `pnpm ui:i18n:sync` pass after translators run; that is outside this PR. ### Notes for the merging maintainer This PR is built on `feat/stack-rebased-20260604`, which carries the prerequisite `skillWorkshop` keys in `en.ts` and the `setSkillWorkshopMode` reducer. `origin/main` does not have those yet (`git show origin/main:ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts | grep skillWorkshop` returns nothing), so this PR should land as part of the same forward-port as the rest of that stack, or the prerequisite commits should be brought in first. No conflict expected with the current `origin/main` locale bundles because the prerequisite `en.ts` and `app-render.ts` changes are the same ones this PR is wired against. ## Related - Memory/QMD: isolate mcporter sidecars per agent #79745 (memory fix PR — i18n split out) - qmd-manager leaks XDG_CONFIG_HOME / XDG_CACHE_HOME to all child spawns, breaking mcporter ≥ 0.10 integration #79847 (underlying qmd/mcporter bug)
target.author: syu8384

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
